blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Common Newborn Dermatoses?
Common newborn dermatoses ส่วนใหญ่ benign + self-limited — แต่การแยก 'common' จาก 'dangerous' คือหน้าที่สำคัญ. 6 ประเด็นหลัก: (1) ETN vs herpes/impetigo: Wright stain eosinophils = ETN, ไม่ต้อง workup ติดเชื้อ; (2) Acropustulosis palms/soles: rule out scabies ก่อนทุกครั้ง — scabies นำหน้า ~50%; (3) Subcutaneous fat necrosis: benign appearance แต่ซ่อน hypercalcemia → renal injury; ต้อง monitor Ca²⁺ ต่อเนื่อง; (4) Neonatal acne ≠ infantile acne: ต่างกันที่อายุ, pathogenesis (Malassezia), treatment; (5) Early emollient ใน at-risk infants อาจป้องกัน AD; (6) Dermal melanocytosis ≠ bruising — เอกสาร lesion ทันทีที่พบ เพื่อป้องกัน confusion กับ child abuse.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
แยก ETN จาก transient neonatal pustular melanosis, miliaria, EPF, และ infectious causes (herpes, impetigo, candidiasis)
รู้ 3 รูปแบบของ miliaria: crystallina, rubra, profunda — ต่างกันที่ level ของ eccrine duct obstruction
อธิบาย acropustulosis of infancy: clinical features, ความสัมพันธ์กับ scabies, treatment (topical corticosteroids; avoid dapsone ใน G6PD deficiency)
Neonatal cephalic pustulosis (neonatal acne): etiology (Malassezia), ต่างจาก infantile acne อย่างไร, treatment options
อธิบาย seborrheic dermatitis ใน neonate: distribution, Malassezia role ที่ยังไม่ชัดเจน, treatment (antifungal vs steroid); ห้ามใช้ olive oil
รู้ pathogenesis ของ atopic dermatitis: filaggrin mutations, Th2 bias, Staph superantigen, food allergy role; treatment step-up ใน infants
แยก diaper dermatitis: irritant vs Candida vs bacterial (impetigo, SSSS, Strep, syphilis) vs Langerhans cell histiocytosis vs zinc deficiency (acrodermatitis enteropathica)
อธิบาย subcutaneous fat necrosis: clinical features, hypercalcemia mechanism (local vitamin D production), management, DDx vs sclerema neonatorum
รู้ Harlequin color change: benign, hypothalamic immaturity → asymmetric sympathetic tone; ต่างจาก harlequin ichthyosis
อธิบาย dermal melanocytosis (Mongolian spots): pathogenesis, prognosis, ความสำคัญของการ document เพื่อป้องกัน child abuse confusion
รู้ aplasia cutis congenita: hair collar sign → MRI CNS; associated syndromes (Adams-Oliver, Bart syndrome EB variant); treatment
รู้ cutis marmorata: benign transient vs persistent (Down syndrome, trisomy 18) vs cutis marmorata telangiectatica congenita
1. Transient Pustular and Vesicular Eruptions — The Big Five
กลุ่มนี้เป็น benign inflammatory conditions ที่ appear + resolve เองโดยไม่ต้องรักษา แต่ต้องแยกจาก infectious causes ก่อนเสมอ. Wright stain ช่วย differentiate ได้รวดเร็วที่ bedside.
1.1 Erythema Toxicum Neonatorum (ETN)
Incidence: 7–41% ของ newborns; พบบ่อยกว่าใน white infants ที่ GA สูง + BW สูง
Timing + Morphology: DOL 1–3; irregularly shaped erythematous macules → overlying vesicles/pustules; most commonly trunk; palms/soles spared; persist 1–2 weeks then resolve without sequelae
Diagnosis: clinical ± Wright/Giemsa stain → eosinophils predominant; biopsy: intraepidermal vesicles filled with eosinophils + mixed intradermal infiltrate around superficial pilosebaceous follicle

Etiology (theory): cutaneous immunologic response to microbial colonization of hair follicles after birth; ↑ inflammatory mediators, ↑ eosinophil + macrophage activity (Marchini G et al. Pediatr Res 2005;58:613)
DDx: transient neonatal pustular melanosis, miliaria, EPF, acropustulosis, folliculitis, candidiasis, impetigo, herpes
Treatment: ไม่จำเป็น — reassure parents; spontaneous resolution สัปดาห์เดียว; prolonged/recurrent courses rare
1.2 Transient Neonatal Pustular Melanosis
Demographics: more common in African-American infants; present at birth

3 Clinical Phases: (1) fragile superficial 2–10 mm pustules at birth (forehead, chin, lower back, shins) — often wiped off during initial cleaning; (2) fine collarette of scale around resolving pustule; (3) hyperpigmented brown macules at pustule sites — may persist several months
Phases 1–2: last 1–2 weeks; hyperpigmentation: several months. Pustule scraping → Wright stain → neutrophil predominance (ต่างจาก ETN ที่เป็น eosinophils)
Etiology unknown; DDx: ETN, miliaria, acropustulosis, candidiasis, impetigo, herpes
Treatment: ไม่จำเป็น; บอกครอบครัวว่า hyperpigmentation ใช้เวลา months ถึง resolve
1.3 Eosinophilic Pustular Folliculitis (EPF)

Infantile form: relatively rare; pruritic follicular lesions typically on extremities or scalp; truncal involvement described; crops อยู่ days–weeks ก่อน resolve; ไม่ annular (ต่างจาก adult EPF); can occur DOL 1 แต่ tends to present later than ETN
Diagnosis: clinical; persistence >several weeks แยก EPF จาก ETN; peripheral eosinophilia supportive; histology: eosinophilic follicular infiltrate
Etiology unknown; Treatment: symptomatic — emollients, topical corticosteroids, antihistamines; refractory cases: topical indomethacin (Hashizume S et al. J Dermatol 2014;41:196)
1.4 Acropustulosis of Infancy
Classic: pruritic vesiculopustular rash on hands and feet → spread to wrists + calves; first described 1979; most common in African-American, Hispanic, Asian children; age <1 year
Crops of intensely pruritic vesicles + pustules → persist ~2 weeks → resolve; frequent recurrences, decreasing in intensity; eventual complete resolution 2–3 years of age
Scabies association: scabies precedes ~50% of cases; also linked to coxsackievirus infection (Paloni G et al. Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2013;98:F340)
Biopsy (if obtained): keratinocyte necrolysis + intraepidermal pustules filled with neutrophils + eosinophils

⚠️ Acropustulosis: Rule Out and Treat Scabies First
Acropustulosis palms + soles ใน infant → ต้อง rule out scabies ก่อนเสมอ (concurrent infestation must be excluded and treated if present). Treatment ของ episodic acropustulosis: mid-potency to high-potency topical corticosteroids ช่วย discrete episodes แต่ไม่ prevent relapses. หลีกเลี่ยง oral dapsone: risk of hemolysis + methemoglobinemia ใน G6PD-deficient infants (Silverberg NB. Pediatric Skin of Color 2015).
1.5 Milia
Extremely common: 40–50% of newborns; more common with longer gestation + born to multigravidas
Appearance: tiny white monomorphic papules, smooth surface, several to dozens; most common on face (forehead, cheeks, chin). Distinct entity จาก miliaria
Etiology: tiny inclusion cysts ใน epidermis — epidermal tissue invaginates around vellus hair → cyst with keratin-producing wall
Diagnosis: expression → tiny white pearl (keratinocyte debris). DDx: sebaceous hyperplasia (more yellow), miliaria (body, more numerous, distinct variants)
Treatment: ไม่จำเป็น; self-resolves หลายเดือน. Multiple large/persistent milia → rare association with epidermolysis bullosa หรือ orofaciodigital syndrome
2. Eccrine, Sebaceous, and Follicular Disorders
2.1 Miliaria — Eccrine Duct Obstruction

Miliaria = benign rash จาก obstruction of eccrine duct; นำมาซึ่ง sweat accumulation; neonates มี incompletely canalized eccrine ducts → predisposed; occurs first 1–2 weeks of life; resolves within days.

rows: Small clear vesicles easily wiped away; eosinophils few to none on Wright stain; no surrounding erythema
Erythematous papules/papulopustules; head, neck, face, trunk; thought related to overheating; lymphocytes on Wright stain
Mildly inflammatory papular eruption; rare in newborns

blocks.text:
Recurrent Miliaria Rubra → Think Type I Pseudohypoaldosteronism
Recurrent pustular miliaria rubra ใน neonate = rare flag สำหรับ type I pseudohypoaldosteronism: inherited disorder ของ mineralocorticoid resistance → salt-wasting crisis. Potentially fatal. ถ้า miliaria rubra recurs → ตรวจ electrolytes + renin/aldosterone. Prevention ของ miliaria rubra ทั่วไป: avoid overheating.
2.2 Sebaceous Hyperplasia
Seen in ~50% of newborns; smooth white-to-yellow papules around follicles; most prominent at nose + upper lip ('sebaceous triangle')
Etiology: stimulation ของ sebaceous glands โดย maternal androgens in utero (Kanada KN et al. J Pediatr 2012;161:240)
Treatment: ไม่จำเป็น; resolves spontaneously ภายในไม่กี่สัปดาห์แรก
2.3 Neonatal Cephalic Pustulosis (Neonatal Acne)
Erythematous papules + pustules บน cheeks, chin, forehead; within first 30 days; can last several months; comedones absent (ต่างจาก infantile acne ซึ่งมี comedones)
Distinct จาก infantile acne: infantile acne = 3–6 เดือน, lasts years, มี comedones, อาจต้องการ treatment
Wright stain: neutrophil predominance; DDx: miliaria, milia, ETN, candidiasis, folliculitis

Etiology (debated): skin colonization with Malassezia species; Malassezia colonization: 5% ของ newborns ใน week 1 → 30% ใน weeks 2–4; ยังไม่ชัดเจนว่าทำไม Malassezia colonization cause inflammation ใน some แต่ไม่ใช่ others (Ayhan M et al. JAAD 2007;57:1012)
Treatment: ไม่จำเป็น; lesions อาจใช้เวลา months ถึง resolve. Topical imidazole antifungals (clotrimazole, econazole, ketoconazole) อาจ ↓ duration ของ eruption
2.4 Epstein Pearls and Bohn Nodules
Very common benign inclusion cysts ใน mouth of neonates: 1–2 mm smooth white-to-yellow papules
Epstein pearls: found on palate, at midline junction of hard + soft palate; more common in infants born to multigravidas, longer GA, higher BW

Bohn nodules: found on alveolar ridge, most commonly maxillary region; often mistaken for natal teeth → prompts referral; distinguish from natal teeth by location + appearance; natal teeth: usually lower incisors region; X-ray confirms
Etiology: remnants of odontogenic epithelium หรือ salivary glands. Treatment: ไม่จำเป็น; spontaneous resolution weeks–months after birth
3. Inflammatory Skin Disorders
3.1 Seborrheic Dermatitis (SD)

Extremely common; presents 2–4 weeks of age; irregular salmon pink patches with waxy scaling: scalp, forehead, nasolabial folds; diaper area involvement; usually resolves/abates by 1 year of age; severe cases → secondary infection
Strong association with atopic dermatitis (AD): infantile SD may precede AD หรืออยู่ใน same disease spectrum (Alexopoulos A et al. Pediatr Dermatol 2014;31:125)
Etiology: Malassezia overgrowth (adults) → unclear ใน infants; suggested by historical studies (Broberg A et al. Br J Dermatol 1989;120:359)

Treatment: topical antifungals (ketoconazole) = equally efficacious กับ topical hydrocortisone (Cohen S. Arch Dis Child 2004;89:288); ห้ามใช้ olive oil: เป็น medium สำหรับ yeast growth (Siegfried E et al. Arch Pediatr 2012;166:967)
DDx: AD, psoriasis, Langerhans cell histiocytosis (rare แต่ serious), superficial fungal infection
3.2 Atopic Dermatitis (AD)
AD = chronic, relapsing condition of defective skin barrier + immune alteration; affects 15–20% ของ population; onset in infancy in >50% ของ cases.
Infantile AD — Distribution
Most common areas: cheeks (± irritant dermatitis จาก saliva), neck, flexural folds, wrists, ankles, nipples; infants: extensor surfaces + trunk more than flexural; diaper area + nasal tip spared
Findings: erythema, scaling, lichenification, excoriation; itching is the predominant symptom; superinfection common; overlap with SD significant
Pathogenesis
Filaggrin (FLG) mutations: loss-of-function variants → ↓ skin barrier function → early-onset + persistent AD (Palmer CN et al. Nat Genet 2006;38:441; Brown SJ et al. JACI 2008;121:940)
Th2 bias: ↑ IL-4 → ↑ IgE + eosinophilia + mast cell proliferation + histamine release
Staphylococcus aureus: nearly all AD patients carry S. aureus; staphylococcal exotoxins as superantigens → stimulate T-cell responses (Leung DY. JACI 1995;96:302)

Food allergy: relevant ใน minority only; Cochrane review: maternal dietary antigen avoidance during pregnancy → no impact ใน first 18 months + may ↑ preterm birth/↓ BW (Kramer MS et al. Evid Based Child Health 2014;9:447)
Breastfeeding: first 4 months ↓ incidence + severity of AD ใน high-risk infants; prolonged breastfeeding ไม่มีผล (Flohr C et al. Br J Dermatol 2011;165:1280)
AD Treatment Stepladder
Maintenance: gentle bathing + bland emollient ≥2x daily + after bathing; limit soap to soiled areas; avoid long hot baths
Active rash (first-line): topical corticosteroids class 6–7 (hydrocortisone 1–2.5%); second-line: topical calcineurin inhibitors (tacrolimus/pimecrolimus) — NOT approved <2 years
Infection: topical/systemic antibiotics when concurrent skin infection; dilute bleach baths (preventive for Staph colonization) (Huang JT et al. Pediatrics 2009;123:e808)
Itch/sleep: sedating antihistamine (diphenhydramine, hydroxyzine) ถ้า age-appropriate + sleep disruption

Early Emollient Use อาจป้องกัน AD ใน At-Risk Infants
Evidence suggests early emollient use ใน infants at risk of AD (family history, FLG mutations) may prevent later disease — rationale: restore barrier function before allergen sensitization occurs. Avery 2024 key point. Diligent moisturization ≥2x daily + always after bathing คือ cornerstone ของ AD prevention + maintenance.

Severe Recalcitrant Eczema + Failure to Thrive → Rule Out Immunodeficiency
Severe AD + recurrent unusual infections → นึกถึง: Wiskott-Aldrich syndrome, chronic granulomatous disease, hyper-IgE syndrome, SCID. AD alone ไม่ cause failure to thrive — FTT + eczema + infections = red flag สำหรับ primary immunodeficiency.
3.3 Diaper Dermatitis (DD)

DD = most common skin condition of infants; primarily an irritant process: friction + occlusion + maceration + chemical/biologic irritants (urine, feces, soaps, wipes) → barrier breakdown → perpetuating cycle.

Clinical: erythema + mild scaling of gluteal crease, buttocks, convex surfaces pubic area + perianal rim; relative sparing of skinfolds (protected from diaper contact); erosions/plaques ใน severe cases; Jacquet dermatitis: noduloerosive form associated with cloth diaper use
DDx ของ Diaper Rash — สิ่งที่ต้องแยก

rows: Convex surfaces, spares skinfolds; responds to barrier cream + frequent changes
Bright red, well-demarcated patches + satellite lesions; involves skinfolds; treat with anti-Candida agent
Scattered bullae + vesicles in diaper area; often more widespread; culture + treat
Brightly erythematous perianal patch; rectal swab culture
Diaper rash + other stigmata; RPR/VDRL + dark field
Persistent petechiae/purpura in diaper area; does not respond to antifungal/steroid; biopsy
Zinc deficiency; perioral + acral + diaper; concurrent oral lesions + alopecia; check zinc

blocks.text: Treatment: superabsorbent disposable diapers > cloth diapers at preventing DD (Lane AT et al. Am J Dis Child 1990;144:315); zinc oxide ointment / petroleum jelly barrier pastes; remove zinc with mineral oil; avoid harsh soaps; if no Candida: low-potency topical steroid; if Candida: anti-Candida agent added
4. Vascular and Pigmentary Conditions
4.1 Harlequin Color Change

Rare but benign; first reported 1952; abrupt deep vascular red color change to one side of body + contralateral blanching + sharp midline demarcation; can involve entire body or localized area; more common in premature infants
Etiology: immaturity of hypothalamic control center → asymmetric control of sympathetic peripheral vascular tone → skin color change with sharp midline demarcation
Diagnosis: clinical; rapid resolution norm; treatment: none; recognition + parental education important. Unrelated to harlequin ichthyosis — ชื่อเหมือนกัน แต่คนละโรคสิ้นเชิง
4.2 Cutis Marmorata

Common, particularly in premature infants; netlike violaceous reticular blanching pattern affecting extremities > trunk; exaggerated with cooling + resolves with warming; transient shifts in skin blood flow
Resolves by ~1 month of age; persistent past 1 month → associated with Down syndrome, trisomy 18, Cornelia de Lange syndrome
Cutis marmorata telangiectatica congenita (CMTC): persists after warming + localized + asymmetric; distinctive vascular anomaly → limb atrophy association
DDx: livedo reticularis: clear vascular pattern related to vascular inflammation/vasculitis — rare ใน infants; treatment: warming; persistent/unresponsive to warming → workup underlying cause
4.3 Dermal Melanocytosis (Mongolian Spots)

Exceedingly common ใน African-American, Asian, Hispanic children; well-demarcated deep blue to gray pigmented macules + patches; most common: buttocks + low back; usually isolated, no systemic association
Rare case reports: DM + inborn errors of metabolism (GM1 gangliosidosis); phakomatosis pigmentovascularis (twin spotting)
Etiology: excessive dermal melanocytes; bluish-gray color = Tyndall effect (light reflection off dermal-based melanocytes)

Prognosis: 42% disappear by 1 year of age (Gupta D et al. Indian J Dermatol Venereol Leprol 2013;79:469); features predicting persistence past 1 year: multiple dark/large patches + locations other than sacrum; most fade by several years; laser treatment NOT recommended

IMPORTANT: Document Dermal Melanocytosis ทันทีที่พบ
Dermal melanocytosis อาจถูก confused กับ bruising จาก child abuse. Bruising: color changes over time (blue-green → brown) + tender to palpation. Dermal melanocytosis: stable blue-gray, non-tender. ดังนั้น document DM เมื่อพบครั้งแรก (location, size, color) เพื่อป้องกัน unjust accusation ของ non-accidental injury ในอนาคต.
5. Subcutaneous and Structural Conditions
5.1 Subcutaneous Fat Necrosis of the Newborn

Affects full-term infants within first several weeks after birth; single or multiple poorly demarcated tender firm nodules + plaques with dusky reddish-purple hue; located at fat pads: buttocks, thighs, arms, face, shoulders
Calcification: can occur → associated with hypercalcemia
Biopsy: subcutaneous granulomatous infiltration + multinucleated giant cells + damaged lipocytes containing characteristic needle-shaped clefts

⚠️ Subcutaneous Fat Necrosis: Monitor Calcium — Risk of Severe Hypercalcemia
Mechanism: soft tissue necrosis + inflammation → local production of vitamin D → hypercalcemia → nephrocalcinosis → renal injury. Management: (1) Serial serum calcium levels (even after skin resolution); (2) Assess for renal injury (creatinine, renal ultrasound for nephrocalcinosis); (3) Treat hypercalcemia if present (fluids, furosemide, consider bisphosphonates — controlled trials still lacking). Lesions resolve weeks–months; DDx: sclerema neonatorum (diffuse progressive skin hardening — life-threatening panniculitis; very different course).
Etiology: intrauterine/perinatal trauma; associated with maternal cocaine use, hypothermia, meconium aspiration, perinatal asphyxia (Rubin G et al. Clin Case Rep 2015;3:1017)
5.2 Aplasia Cutis Congenita (ACC)

Rare congenital skin defect; most often localized + isolated on scalp; localized loss of skin of variable thickness → open ulceration หรือ healed atrophic scar; rarely associated with underlying skeletal abnormalities
Hair collar sign: ring of darker, longer hair surrounding lesion = ↑ risk of underlying neurodevelopmental abnormality → CNS imaging required

Associated conditions: Adams-Oliver syndrome (extensive ACC + limb hypoplasia); Bart syndrome (EB variant = congenital localized absence of skin); Goltz syndrome (focal dermal hypoplasia) (Tollefson MM. NeoReviews 2012;13:e285)
Etiology: unclear; theories: intrauterine trauma, vascular abnormalities, intrauterine infection, maternal medications
Treatment: lesions heal without intervention but scarring present; large lesions may need surgical closure; scar revision available later; identify + manage underlying neurodevelopmental abnormality
6. NICU Practical — Common Newborn Dermatoses ที่ต้องรู้

ETN on first rounds: erythematous macules/vesicles/pustules DOL 1–3, palms/soles spared → clinical diagnosis ETN; ถ้าไม่ชัดเจน: scrape pustule + Wright stain → eosinophils = ETN; ไม่ต้อง sepsis workup; reassure family; self-resolves

Papulopustular palms + soles: rule out scabies ก่อนเสมอ — ตรวจผิวหนัง + ซักประวัติ household contacts; ถ้ามี acropustulosis: mid-potency topical corticosteroid สำหรับ discrete episodes; avoid dapsone ใน G6PD-deficient infants
Subcutaneous fat necrosis: firm nodules/plaques ที่ fat pads ใน term infant หลัง perinatal stress → serial Ca²⁺ monitoring แม้หลัง lesions resolve; ตรวจ creatinine + renal ultrasound ถ้า Ca²⁺ ↑

Diaper rash ที่ไม่ตอบสนอง: ไม่ดีขึ้นหลัง barrier cream + frequency change → นึกถึง Candida (satellite lesions), bacterial (culture), Langerhans cell histiocytosis (biopsy), acrodermatitis enteropathica (ตรวจ zinc level)

Neonatal acne: cheeks/chin/forehead, no comedones, DOL <30 → reassure; ถ้า parents ต้องการ treatment: topical ketoconazole ↓ duration; อย่าสับสนกับ infantile acne (3–6 เดือน, มี comedones, ต้องการ treatment มากกว่า)
Mongolian spots เมื่อ admit: document ทันทีในเวชระเบียน: location, size, color, photograph ถ้าทำได้ → ป้องกัน confusion กับ non-accidental injury ในอนาคต
Miliaria rubra recurs: avoid overheating; ถ้า recurrent + electrolyte abnormality → type I pseudohypoaldosteronism workup
Hair collar sign ที่ scalp: CNS imaging ทันที — อาจมี underlying neurodevelopmental abnormality associated กับ aplasia cutis congenita
7. 5 Bedside Pearls — สำหรับพรุ่งนี้

PEARL 1 — Palms + Soles Pustules: Rule Out Scabies First
Papulopustular lesions on palms and/or soles ในเดือนแรก = rule out scabies ก่อนเสมอ — scabies นำหน้า acropustulosis ~50%. ถ้า scabies present → treat ก่อน แล้ว manage acropustulosis episodes ด้วย mid-high potency topical corticosteroids. หลีกเลี่ยง dapsone ใน G6PD deficiency: hemolysis + methemoglobinemia risk.

PEARL 2 — Subcutaneous Fat Necrosis: Benign Appearance ≠ No Monitoring
Firm nodules ที่ fat pads ใน term infant หลัง perinatal stress = subcutaneous fat necrosis; ดู benign แต่ซ่อน hypercalcemia จาก local vitamin D production. Monitor serum calcium ต่อเนื่องแม้หลัง skin lesions resolve — hypercalcemia อาจ delayed. ถ้า Ca²⁺ ↑ → ตรวจ nephrocalcinosis (Tuddenham E et al. BMJ Case Rep 2015).

PEARL 3 — ETN: Eosinophils = Reassurance, Not Infection
ETN: erythematous macules/vesicles DOL 1–3, palms/soles spared. ถ้า parent/nursing staff กังวล: scrape pustule → Wright stain → eosinophils = ETN = benign; ไม่ต้อง sepsis workup, ไม่ต้อง antibiotics. Neutrophils predominant = transient neonatal pustular melanosis; vesicular + irregular distribution → consider herpes → HSV PCR.

PEARL 4 — Seborrheic Dermatitis: ห้ามใช้ Olive Oil
Seborrheic dermatitis (cradle cap + diaper area): topical ketoconazole = equally efficacious กับ hydrocortisone → preferred ถ้า antifungal cause suspected. ห้ามแนะนำ olive oil: เป็น nutrient medium สำหรับ Malassezia/yeast → ทำให้แย่ลง (Siegfried E et al. 2012). Ketoconazole ≤2 weeks resolves most cases ใน infants.

PEARL 5 — Mongolian Spots: Document on Day 1
Dermal melanocytosis (Mongolian spots) พบมากใน Asian/African-American/Hispanic infants — document ทันทีเมื่อ admit: เขียนใน chart + photograph. Tyndall effect → stable blue-gray, non-tender, non-changing. Bruising: changes color over time + tender. 42% fade ภายใน 1 ปี; laser treatment ไม่แนะนำ. Rare association: GM1 gangliosidosis.
8. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

Monteagudo B et al. Pediatr Dermatol 2012;29:166: Prospective study ของ ETN epidemiology — incidence 7–41%; more common in white, higher GA, higher BW newborns; supports immune-based hair follicle theory.
Marchini G et al. Pediatr Res 2005;58:613: ETN เป็น innate immune response to commensal microbes penetrating neonatal skin at hair follicle openings; ↑ HMGB-1 expression in ETN lesions.
Paloni G et al. Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2013;98:F340: Acropustulosis of infancy — clinical features, scabies relationship, management; dapsone less favored due to G6PD risk.

Ayhan M et al. JAAD 2007;57:1012: Malassezia colonization of neonate skin — 5% week 1 → 30% weeks 2–4; relationship with neonatal cephalic pustulosis debated; colonization rate similar in affected vs unaffected infants.

Bernier V et al. Arch Dermatol 2002;138:215: Prospective study of Malassezia skin colonization in neonates — relationship with neonatal cephalic pustulosis explored; topical antifungals effective in shortening eruption duration.

Alexopoulos A et al. Pediatr Dermatol 2014;31:125: Retrospective analysis of infantile SD and AD relationship — strong association; infantile SD may precede or be on same spectrum as AD; relevant for counseling atopic families.

Cohen S. Arch Dis Child 2004;89:288: Should we treat infantile seborrheic dermatitis with antifungals or topical steroids? — ketoconazole equally efficacious as hydrocortisone; supports antifungal as first-line.

Palmer CN et al. Nat Genet 2006;38:441: Common loss-of-function variants of filaggrin (FLG) = major predisposing factor for atopic dermatitis; foundation for understanding AD pathogenesis and early prevention strategies.

Kramer MS & Kakuma R. Evid Based Child Health 2014;9:447: Cochrane review: maternal dietary antigen avoidance during pregnancy/lactation — no impact on AD development in first 18 months; may ↑ preterm birth/↓ birth weight.

Flohr C et al. Br J Dermatol 2011;165:1280: ISAAC phase 2 study: prolonged breastfeeding has NO protective effect on childhood eczema; first 4 months breastfeeding beneficial but prolonged duration does not add further benefit.

Huang JT et al. Pediatrics 2009;123:e808: Treatment of S. aureus colonization in AD decreases disease severity — dilute bleach baths reduce Staph burden; addresses superantigen-driven T-cell responses.

Lane AT et al. Am J Dis Child 1990;144:315: Evaluation of diapers in newborns — superabsorbent disposable diapers better at preventing diaper dermatitis than cloth diapers; foundation for current diaper recommendation.

Kanada KN et al. J Pediatr 2012;161:240: Prospective study of cutaneous findings in US newborns — sebaceous hyperplasia ~50%; correlation with race, ethnicity, gestational status; updated classification and nomenclature.

Rubin G et al. Clin Case Rep 2015;3:1017: Subcutaneous fat necrosis of the newborn — case series; perinatal asphyxia + therapeutic hypothermia association; hypercalcemia monitoring critical; bisphosphonate use described.

Tuddenham E et al. BMJ Case Rep 2015: Subcutaneous fat necrosis causing neonatal hypercalcaemia — mechanism: local vitamin D production by activated macrophages in necrotic fat; renal complications if untreated; serial Ca²⁺ monitoring mandatory.

Gupta D & Thappa DM. Indian J Dermatol Venereol Leprol 2013;79:469: Mongolian spots epidemiology — 42% fade by 1 year; persistence associated with dark/large/extra-sacral patches; laser not recommended.

Tollefson MM. NeoReviews 2012;13:e285: Aplasia cutis congenita comprehensive review — hair collar sign indicates CNS imaging; associated syndromes; treatment approach; Adams-Oliver and Bart syndrome links.
9. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: แยก ETN จาก transient neonatal pustular melanosis ด้วย Wright stain + clinical features อย่างไร?

ตอบ: ETN: eosinophils predominant บน Wright stain; erythematous macules/vesicles DOL 1–3; trunk > extremities; palms/soles spared; common ใน white infants, higher GA + BW. Transient neonatal pustular melanosis: neutrophils predominant บน Wright stain; present AT birth (ไม่ใช่ DOL 1–3); fragile pustules → collarette of scale → residual hyperpigmented macules (months); common ใน African-American infants. ทั้งสองไม่ต้อง treatment.
Q: แยก 3 types ของ miliaria ด้วย level of obstruction + clinical appearance?

ตอบ: (1) Miliaria crystallina: obstruction within/below stratum corneum → small clear vesicles easily wiped away; no erythema; Wright: few-to-no cells; (2) Miliaria rubra: obstruction at epidermis level → erythematous papules/papulopustules; head/neck/face/trunk; related to overheating; Wright: lymphocytes; (3) Miliaria profunda: obstruction at/below dermal-epidermal junction → mildly inflammatory papular eruption; rare in newborns. Recurrent miliaria rubra → rule out type I pseudohypoaldosteronism (salt-wasting, potentially fatal).
Q: Infant มี firm tender nodules ที่ buttocks + thighs หลัง perinatal asphyxia — diagnose + immediate concern?

ตอบ: Subcutaneous fat necrosis of the newborn: firm poorly demarcated nodules/plaques ที่ fat pads (buttocks, thighs, arms, face, shoulders); dusky reddish-purple hue; associated with perinatal asphyxia, hypothermia, maternal cocaine. Immediate concern: hypercalcemia: local vitamin D production by activated macrophages in necrotic fat → Ca²⁺ ↑ → nephrocalcinosis + renal injury. Action: serial serum calcium monitoring แม้หลัง skin lesions resolve; ตรวจ renal function + renal ultrasound ถ้า Ca²⁺ ↑. DDx: sclerema neonatorum (diffuse progressive hardening, life-threatening).
Q: Neonatal acne vs infantile acne — แยกกันอย่างไร? Malassezia มีบทบาทอย่างไร?

ตอบ: Neonatal acne (neonatal cephalic pustulosis): onset <30 days; erythematous papules/pustules บน cheeks/chin/forehead; ไม่มี comedones; associated with Malassezia colonization; self-limited months; topical imidazoles ↓ duration. Infantile acne: onset 3–6 months; มี open/closed comedones (blackheads/whiteheads); lasts years; may need benzoyl peroxide/retinoids. Malassezia: colonizes skin 5% → 30% ใน weeks 2–4; mechanism of inflammation uncertain — correlation between colonization degree + disease severity ยังไม่ชัดเจน.
Q: Diaper rash ที่ไม่ตอบสนองต่อ zinc oxide + frequent changes — DDx ที่ต้องนึกถึง?

ตอบ: DDx ของ refractory diaper rash: (1) Candida: bright red + satellite lesions + involves skinfolds → topical anti-Candida; (2) Bullous impetigo (Staph): scattered bullae/vesicles → culture + antibiotics; (3) Strep perianal: brightly erythematous perianal patch → rectal swab; (4) Langerhans cell histiocytosis: petechiae/purpura + doesn't respond → biopsy; (5) Acrodermatitis enteropathica (zinc deficiency): perioral + acral + diaper + alopecia → ตรวจ zinc level; (6) Congenital syphilis → RPR/VDRL. Key clue: skin findings in other body areas outside diaper region often identify non-irritant causes.
Q: Hair collar sign บน scalp neonate — ทำอะไรทันที? เกี่ยวข้องกับโรคอะไรบ้าง?

ตอบ: Hair collar sign = ring of darker, longer hair surrounding scalp lesion → CNS imaging ทันที (MRI/CT) — indicates ↑ risk of underlying neurodevelopmental abnormality associated with aplasia cutis congenita (ACC). Associated conditions: (1) Adams-Oliver syndrome: ACC + limb hypoplasia; (2) Bart syndrome: variant of EB — congenital localized absence of skin; (3) Goltz syndrome (focal dermal hypoplasia). Isolated ACC ไม่มี hair collar sign: heals ด้วย scarring ไม่ต้อง CNS imaging; large lesions: may need surgical closure.
Q: ทำไมต้อง document Mongolian spots เมื่อ admit? blue-gray macular lesions in dark-skinned infant จะแยก bruising อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Dermal melanocytosis อาจถูก confused กับ bruising จาก child abuse. Distinction: (1) Mongolian spots: stable blue-gray color → ไม่ change over time, non-tender, Tyndall effect; (2) Bruising: color changes over time (blue-green → yellow-brown) + tender to palpation. Document ทันทีที่ admit: location/size/color/photograph → ป้องกัน unjust accusation ใน future. Prognosis: 42% fade by 1 year; large/dark/extra-sacral = tends to persist; most fade by several years; laser NOT recommended.
Q: Early emollients ใน infants at risk of AD — evidence basis และ mechanism ที่เชื่อว่าเป็นอยู่?

ตอบ: Evidence (Avery Ch94 key point): early emollient use in at-risk infants may prevent later AD development. Mechanism proposed: filaggrin mutations → ↓ skin barrier function → TEWL ↑ + allergen penetration → sensitization → Th2 activation → AD. Early emollients restore barrier before sensitization occurs. Supporting genetics: Palmer CN et al. Nat Genet 2006 (filaggrin loss-of-function = major predisposing factor). In practice: bland emollient ≥2x daily + always after bathing; start เร็วที่สุดใน family history ของ atopy. Topical calcineurin inhibitors (tacrolimus/pimecrolimus) NOT approved <2 years.
Day 95 Preview

Avery Chapter 95 — Vascular Anomalies and Other Cutaneous Congenital Defects
พรุ่งนี้: Vascular Anomalies and Other Cutaneous Congenital Defects (Avery Ch.95) — จากวันนี้ที่เรียน common + transient dermatoses เราจะเข้าสู่ congenital vascular lesions: infantile hemangiomas (IH), vascular malformations (capillary, venous, lymphatic, arteriovenous), port-wine stains, PHACE syndrome, LUMBAR syndrome. Key question: infantile hemangioma vs vascular malformation — ต่างกันที่ natural history, timing, treatment (propranolol for IH); port-wine stain = capillary malformation → Sturge-Weber association.
